title: payment_watcher scan_block does not persist tip after stream exhaustion: tip advances but scan_height lags
description: payment_watcher.rs run_watcher_loop: set_payment_scan_tip() is called inside the block processing loop (line 178), once per block. But if the inner block stream errors mid-range (Err branch at line 165), we break out of the block loop without persisting the tip for partially-scanned blocks. The outer loop then breaks too (line 193). On reconnect, last_scanned_height in memory is correct but the persisted DB tip may lag by up to (scan_end - scan_start) blocks. On process restart, the watcher re-scans those blocks. This is idempotent (activate_subscription_atomic is idempotent) but causes redundant DB load after every crash.
